Version
  • Quantile Regression (QGAM)
  • Quantile Regression (QGAM)
GitHub

Quantile Regression (QGAM)

Quantile GAMs estimate conditional quantile functions rather than conditional means. This is useful when you want to understand how the entire conditional distribution changes with covariates — not just the average.

GAM.jl's QGAM implementation follows Fasiolo et al. (2021), using the extended log-F (ELF) likelihood for smooth, calibrated quantile estimation.

Key Concepts

  • Quantile regression: estimate the τ-th quantile of y|x instead of E[y|x]
  • ELF family: a smooth loss function that approximates the pinball loss
  • Calibration: automatic learning rate selection to control coverage

Interface

Single Quantile

qgam(formula, data; qu=0.5, control=gam_control())

Fits a GAM for a single quantile level qu ∈ (0, 1).

Multiple Quantiles

mqgam(formula, data; qu=[0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9])

Fits all specified quantiles efficiently, sharing basis construction. Returns a MqgamResult from which individual models can be extracted.

Extract Individual Quantile

qdo(mqgam_result, quantile_level)

Extracts a fitted model for a single quantile from an mqgam result.

Families

FamilyDescription
ELFFamily(qu)Extended log-F for quantile qu
ELFLSSFamily(qu)ELF with location-scale-shape for flexible tails

In most cases you do not need to construct these directly — qgam() and mqgam() handle family construction internally.

Examples

Median Regression

using GAM, DataFrames

n = 500
x = range(0, 2π; length=n) |> collect
y = sin.(x) .+ (0.1 .+ 0.3 .* x ./ (2π)) .* randn(n)
df = DataFrame(x=x, y=y)

m = qgam(@gam_formula(y ~ s(x, k=20, bs=:cr)), df; qu=0.5)

Multiple Quantiles

fits = mqgam(@gam_formula(y ~ s(x, k=20, bs=:cr)), df;
    qu=[0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9])

# Extract individual quantile models
m10 = qdo(fits, 0.1)
m50 = qdo(fits, 0.5)
m90 = qdo(fits, 0.9)

Poisson-like Counts with Quantile Regression

using Distributions

mu = exp.(1.0 .+ 0.5 .* sin.(x))
y = Float64.([rand(Poisson(m)) for m in mu])
df = DataFrame(x=x, y=y)

# Quantile regression on count data
m = qgam(@gam_formula(y ~ s(x, k=15, bs=:cr)), df;
    qu=0.75, family=Poisson(), link=LogLink())

Calibration

QGAM includes an automatic calibration step that selects the learning rate (smoothing of the ELF loss) to achieve correct coverage. This is performed internally during fitting. The calibration ensures that the estimated quantile has the correct nominal coverage probability.

See Also

  • Families & Models for ELF family details
